title: Ketan Agrawal death case: Chetan 'left phone' at shop, carried employee’s device —Timeline
content: Pune police are investigating the alleged murder of realtor Ketan Agrawal, with accused Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary reportedly researching killing methods before staging a fall at Lohagad Fort. Chaudhary allegedly used an employee's phone to evade tracking. The duo, who had extensive contact, met days before the incident, which was initially deemed accidental but is now a murder probe.

title: ‘PLA built roads, camps inside India’: Border tribe alleges Chinese land grab in Arunachal
content: Arunachal Pradesh's Nah tribal community has voiced serious concerns over alleged Chinese PLA incursions in Upper Subansiri district, claiming significant portions of their ancestral grazing and hunting lands have been occupied over the past six years. The Nah Welfare Society detailed transgressions at five locations near Taksing, alleging construction of military camps and roads on Indian territory. Local MLA emphasized the need for official verification of these national security-related allegations.

title: 'Still trying to process it': Rajasthan-born Moondra revels in Ireland's India triumph
content: Ireland achieved a historic 2-0 T20I series triumph over reigning world champions India, with debutant Jai Moondra emerging as the unlikely hero. The software engineer from Rajasthan claimed crucial wickets, including Sanju Samson and Shreyas Iyer, to seal a dramatic one-run victory. Moondra, named Player of the Match and Series, expressed disbelief at the monumental achievement.

title: Microsoft under Italy regulator’s scanner over Copilot, designer integration
content: Italy's competition watchdog has launched an investigation into Microsoft over claims it automatically upgraded subscribers to pricier Microsoft 365 plans with new AI features like Copilot and Designer. The authority is concerned customers weren't clearly informed about the AI integration and the resulting price hike, potentially leading to an unfair commercial practice and restricted consumer choice.

title: SUV hits car reversing on Delhi-Dehradun expressway; 4 of Haryana family dead
content: A tragic accident on the Delhi-Dehradun expressway claimed four lives from a Haryana family. Their car, reportedly being reversed after a missed exit, was violently struck from behind by a speeding SUV. The collision, captured on CCTV, was so forceful that the victims' vehicle was dragged and flipped, leaving three others critically injured. Authorities are investigating the incident.

title: Land donated for a park for $10 30 years ago, sold for $10m to build a data center
content: A Texas city sold 87 acres donated in 1999 for a $10 public park to a data center developer for $10 million. Residents, led by Pamela Griffin, are suing to honour the original deed, while Taylor officials say existing zoning permits the data center without resident approval. The project promises $30 million in tax revenue but raises concerns over noise, water use and property values.

title: 'I don't have any more fight left in me': Stokes reveals reason behind shock retirement
content: England captain Ben Stokes has revealed the profound emotional toll of the Ashes defeat as the primary reason for his international retirement.  He confessed to his wife that he felt "no more fight" left to overcome the disappointment.  Stokes cited overwhelming emotional, physical, and mental exhaustion, accumulated over years of demanding cricket, as the driving force behind his decision to step away from the game he loves.

title: Jeff Bezos vs Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: 'Papa, save me. I’m dying': JEE aspirant's last call before mysterious death in Kota
content: A 17-year-old engineering aspirant from Araria died in Kota after being found critically injured. Moments before her death, she made a desperate plea to her father from the hospital, crying for help. The family is in shock, unaware of how she sustained her injuries. The circumstances surrounding her tragic demise remain mysterious, with police now investigating the incident.

title: 24-hour heat stress: Day ‘feel’ 50°C, night warmest in 2 years in Delhi
content: Delhi sweltered under extreme heat stress, with 'feels like' temperatures soaring past 50 degrees Celsius and night temperatures hitting a two-year high for June. A heatwave intensified the oppressive conditions, exacerbated by high humidity. This prolonged exposure poses significant health risks, with monsoon rains still delayed, offering little immediate relief to the capital's residents.

title: The price of paradise: What happens when offbeat travel destinations go mainstream
content: Once a cherished secret, India's offbeat travel destinations are rapidly becoming mainstream. What began as a quiet escape for a few now faces overcrowding, mirroring global trends. As infrastructure improves and social media fuels aspiration, the very charm of these places is threatened. Travelers grapple with the desire for authentic experiences versus their role in accelerating discovery and its consequences.

title: Vat Purnima 2026: Date, time, puja rituals and significance
content: Married Hindu women across the nation are observing Vat Purnima on June 29, 2026, a significant festival dedicated to the well-being and longevity of their husbands. The day involves a strict fast and prayers offered to the sacred banyan tree, symbolizing immortality and divine presence.

title: ‘Donation first, not nation first’: Akhilesh Yadav’s broadside against BJP on Ayodhya row
content: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav has sharply criticized the BJP government, accusing them of religious insensitivity regarding alleged Ram temple donation theft and playing with youth's future through repeated exam paper leaks. He highlighted concerns over women's safety and unemployment, vowing to reform the recruitment system if his party gains power. Yadav also alleged conspiracies against MPs post-election defeat.

title: Seika, Kyungbock crowned champions as NBA Rising Stars Invitational ends on a high
content: Over six days in Singapore, the NBA Rising Stars Invitational evolved into far more than a high-school basketball tournament. With Asia's best young talent, NBA and WNBA legends, packed stands and a growing ecosystem around the game, the event offered a glimpse into where basketball in the region is headed.

title: China blacklists 20 Japanese entities after imposing export curbs on 10 US firms
content: China has expanded its technology and national security restrictions by adding 20 Japanese entities, including a defense studies institute, to its export control list. This move, barring dual-use item exports, follows similar actions against 10 US firms last week. Beijing states the decision aims to protect national security and uphold non-proliferation commitments amidst rising geopolitical tensions.

title: AP EAMCET result 2026 expected soon at cets.apsche.ap.gov.in: Steps to download scorecards
content: The AP EAMCET 2026 result is expected to be announced shortly, ending the wait for engineering, agriculture and pharmacy aspirants across Andhra Pradesh. Candidates will be able to download their rank cards online before the counselling process begins, which will include registration, document verification, choice filling and seat allotment for undergraduate admissions.

title: Colour-coded crime: Tamil Nadu cops rank 15k sex offenders by risk
content: Tamil Nadu police have launched 'Spectrum' in the south zone to classify and track sexual offenders based on risk. This project categorizes around 15,000 individuals into eight color-coded groups, from high-risk 'Red' for gang rape accused to 'Silver' for juveniles. The initiative aims for targeted monitoring, especially of repeat offenders and cybercriminals, utilizing biometrics and social media surveillance to enhance public safety.

title: Arunachal flood toll climbs to 3; Assam faces rail disruption
content: Flash floods in Arunachal Pradesh's Keyi Panyor district have claimed three lives, with another NEEPCO employee's body recovered. Heavy rains have also devastated Assam's Dhemaji district, affecting over 15,000 people and disrupting train services due to a damaged railway bridge. Landslides have been reported across several Arunachal districts, impacting thousands. Assam's Chief Minister is actively overseeing relief efforts.

title: 'They outplayed us': Shreyas Iyer joins Pant in unwanted club after Ireland shock
content: Ireland secured a historic 2-0 T20I series win over India, marking a tough start for captain Shreyas Iyer. India faltered in their chase of 155, falling short by one run despite a late surge. Iyer acknowledged Ireland's superior understanding of the conditions, admitting India's batting unit struggled to adapt. The defeat also ended India's impressive 16-series winning streak.

title: Pune man wanted to kill 15,000 with poison pills at Muharram rally in Mumbai, say cops
content: A 39-year-old man, arrested for allegedly distributing poison-filled pills during a Muharram procession, reportedly arrived in Mumbai two weeks prior and ordered a large quantity of zinc phosphide. Police seized nearly 15,000 pills, believed to be assembled in his hotel room, intended to harm thousands. Two individuals who consumed the pills have come forward, displaying poisoning symptoms.

title: MLB trade rumors: Blue Jays could part ways with $110 million star by trade deadline
content: The Blue Jays entered 2026 expecting another October run after coming within reach of a championship against the Los Angeles Dodgers. Instead, Toronto has spent most of the season below .500, turning a roster built to contend into one of the deadline's most closely watched evaluations. At 39-44, the club remains within striking distance of an American League Wild Card berth because of a tightly packed playoff race. Yet the same standings that keep Toronto alive also force the front office to confront a difficult possibility.

title: Assam rail bridge partly collapses after heavy rain; train services suspended, villages cut off
content: Heavy rainfall and severe riverbank erosion have led to the partial collapse of a railway bridge over the Simen River in Assam's Dhemaji district, suspending train operations. Meanwhile, a separate iron bridge connecting Kemi and Oyan was washed away by floodwaters, disrupting connectivity and raising concerns for students and farmers. Authorities are monitoring the situation and arranging alternative transport.

title: FIFA WC: Stephen Eustaquio's late stunner sends Canada into historic last 16
content: Canada secured a historic 1-0 victory over South Africa with a stunning 92nd-minute volley from Stephen Eustaquio at SoFi Stadium. This marks Canada's first-ever knockout stage win in World Cup history. Despite South Africa's strong defense, Eustaquio's late heroics propelled Canada into the Round of 16, where they will face either the Netherlands or Morocco. The win was a significant moment for the nation's football program.

title: 'The Family Man' Season 4 OTT release: When and where to watch spy thriller's next chapter
content: The Family Man Season 4 is officially in the works and will stream on Amazon Prime Video. Creator Raj Nidimoru confirmed that Seasons 3 and 4 were always planned as one continuous story, with Srikant Tiwari's personal journey deliberately left incomplete. The writing for the new season is already underway.

title: All Sikh MLAs, ministers to appear before Akal Takht today: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann
content: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann announced that all Sikh AAP MLAs and ministers will appear before the Akal Takht on Monday to present the government's stance on the new anti-sacrilege law. Mann reiterated his denial of involvement in a controversial sacrilege video, calling it fake and identifying a Canada-based individual as the alleged mastermind. He accused opposition parties of politicizing religious matters for electoral gain.

title: 'Didn't play well against good teams': Harmanpreet minces no words after WC exit
content: The loss meant India failed to qualify for the semi-finals despite posting 170 for four after being asked to bat.

title: Tilak Varma enters unwanted record books after slowest-ever T20I fifty against Ireland
content: Tilak Varma's resilient half-century was a lone bright spot for India in their narrow one-run loss to Ireland, despite becoming one of the slowest Indian fifties in T20Is.  Walking in during a top-order collapse, Varma battled tough conditions to reach his milestone.  Ultimately, Ireland secured a historic series win, ending India's impressive streak.

title: House of the Dragon S3: Daemon and Rhaenyra scene with Daenerys reference goes viral
content: Daenerys Targaryen, the iconic Mother of Dragons from 'Game of Thrones', is making waves in 'House of the Dragon' lore. A recent clip features Daemon Targaryen recounting a prophetic vision at Harrenhal, describing a silver-haired girl with dragons, strongly hinting at Daenerys. This builds on a previous vision at a weirwood tree, further linking the prequel's events to the future of Westeros and Game of Thrones.

title: Pune fort murder: Siya’s ‘sitting down’ signal was also meant to ensure her safety, say police
content: Siya Goyal and her alleged lover had decided that she would sit down either to drink water or on the pretext of tying her shoelaces; sitting down was the signal, the official

title: I don't have wings, so no question of anybody clipping them: Fadnavis, asserts support of BJP leaders
content: Devendra Fadnavis also dismissed the political buzz triggered after he and former ally-turned-rival, Uddhav Thackeray, travelled on the same flight from Mumbai to Nagpur

title: Elephant killings continue in death zone of Kerala
content: Human-induced elephant deaths in the Parambikulam-Munnar–Malayattoor forest sector are back in focus after the death of a makhna elephant in the Malayattoor forests, reportedly after chewing on fruit stuffed with explosives

title: CM Sukhu carries ‘Sarkar Gaon Ke Dwar’ to Himachal’s most inaccessible region, announces key benefits for Bara Bhangal
content: The Chief Minister announced a monthly pension of ₹1,500 for all eligible women in Bara Bhangal and said the region would be developed into an eco-tourism destination.

title: Young athlete’s organs will give others a second chance at life
content: A postgraduate from St. Thomas College, budding actor, and Kho Kho player declared brain dead after road accident; family chooses organ donation

title: Govt. rejects Pakistan’s allegations linking India to Karachi attack
content: Official spokesperson of the External Affairs Ministry Randhir Jaiswal said, “Pakistan would do better to look inwards, take credible actions (against) the terror infrastructure on its territory”.

title: IMD says wait for monsoon may be over in 5-6 days even as Delhi, Uttar Pradesh reel under heat
content: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) said in a release that conditions are favourable for further advance of the southwest monsoon into some more parts of the North Arabian Sea and some areas of Uttar Pradesh and Uttarakhand, among other States, during the next two to three days.

title: Kerala monsoon onset: Why southwest monsoon arrival matters for India
content: The southwest monsoon onset in Kerala not only impacts local weather patterns but also plays a crucial role in shaping India's food security, agricultural GDP, and water supply across the nation.

title: LGBTQIA+ community raises voice against new amendment Act at Chennai Pride
content: Apart from thousands of people who gathered at the march from Chennai, a few also joined from other parts of the world

title: Pride flag hoisted in Chennai amid calls for inclusion and action
content: Beyond the celebrations, many who took part in the event expressed their disappointment with the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act, 2026.

title: Pedestrian rights in focus as Kochi’s footpaths remain inaccessible
content: Recently, the Kerala High Court ruled against the proposed construction of 20 kiosks in the Queen’s Walkway in Kochi, citing the pedestrians’ right of unobstructed access to the walkway

title: Sonam Wangchuk begins hunger strike at Jantar Mantar, six students join
content: Activist joins the CJP protest demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan for multiple irregularities in the examination system

title: ‘Blatant loot’: Congress on MoS Agriculture getting subsidy from own ministry
content: In yet another blow to the 'Chanda-Chor' RSS-BJP's claims of zero tolerance for corruption, Union Minister of State for Agriculture Bhagirath Choudhary approved a ₹99.03 lakh subsidy for his own project, alleges Congress leader Pawan Khera

title: Vanajeevi Ramaiah’s green legacy finds place in Intermediate first year Botany textbook
content: The textbook describes him as an Indian social worker renowned for his outstanding contribution to social forestry. It notes that he was conferred the ‘Padma Shri’ award in recognition of his extensive afforestation efforts in 2017

title: Bihar on verge of bankruptcy, says Tejashwi
content: While addressing the press at the party office, the Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) leader alleged that the state is facing extreme financial loss due to scams, corruption, revenue loss, widening fiscal deficit and mounting debt

title: MoU signed for indigenous chips in CCTV cameras
content: According to Mindgrove Technologies this is one of the largest commercial integrations of Indian-designed silicon to date

title: Hospital dedicated to geriatric care inaugurated in city
content: Karthik Narayan, founder and managing director of Athulya Senior Care, pointed out that India has only around 20,000 dedicated senior care beds, compared with nearly eight million in China and close to three million in the U.S.

title: 1,747 out-of-school children in Yadgir to be brought back to classrooms through NIOS Mitra
content: NIOS Mitra is a technology-enabled community outreach initiative launched by the Ministry of Education to identify, counsel and enrol OoSCs into the formal education system, particularly those in the 14-18 age group

title: Minister for Cooperatives inspects renovated wholesale market on Bazaar Road in Arakkonam
content: Officials of the Arakkonam Municipality, which executed the ₹6-crore work, say this is the second time the market complex was renovated since 1984

title: Landslide dams river in rain-battered Arunachal, warning for Assam downstream
content: Lower Siang district officials say the possibility of a powerful flash flood if the dam breaks could endanger homes along the Siji river, which becomes the Gai river in Assam

title: MEA issues advisory for citizens undertaking Mansarovar Yatra through private tour operators
content: The Mansarovar Yatra resumed last year after a gap of nearly five years as part of efforts to normalise the relations between India and China

title: Stalin accuses TVK-led government of not addressing power cuts, farmers’ problems
content: According to him, industrial establishments are hesitant to invest in the State due to the deterioration of law and order, increasing sexual abuse cases, narcotic drug menace, and robberies

title: Villupuram MP reviews Central government schemes being implemented in the district at DISHA meeting
content: The allocation of funds under each scheme, the ongoing works, and the progress made, besides those that were completed, were also reviewed

title: Five-year-old girl, sleeping at home, found dead in a well in Ramanathapuram
content: Around 5.30 a.m. on Sunday, her mother woke up and found that her elder daughter, who had been sleeping beside her, was missing. On her complaint,  the police, along with Fire and Rescue Services, launched a search and found the body  the neighbour’s well. A case of suspicious death has been registered

title: CPI(M) protests inauguration of rafting event amid search for missing person
content: The party alleges that inaugurating the rafting event in a celebratory manner while an intensive search for the missing person was under way nearby was inhuman and shocking

title: Weather LIVE Updates: 9 Missing After Flash Floods In Arunachal Pradesh
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Nine people go missing after flash floods in Arunachal Pradesh's Lower Dibang Valley.

title: 9 Missing As Floods Hit Arunachal, Rescue Boat Swept Away During Operation
content: Torrential monsoon rains, followed by cloudbursts, have triggered severe flooding in the East Siang and Leparada districts of Arunachal Pradesh.

title: Andy Burnham To Outline Vision For UK With Focus On Raising Living Standards
content: Andy Burnham will commit to a 10-year mission to raise living standards through reindustrialisation, housing, infrastructure and reform of utilities.

title: Siya Goyal's 'Secret Call' With Lover 34 Minutes Before Ketan Agarwal Murder
content: The revelation followed an in-depth forensic examination of the mobile handsets of Goyal and Chaudhary, sources said.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Markets Likely To Open Flat As Oil Prices Rise Again
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude rise to $73.39 a barrel, while US WTI traded near $70 a barrel.

title: "Look Inwards": India Slams Pak Over "Baseless" Karachi Attack Claims
content: Pakistan has carried out airstrikes on eastern Afghanistan overnight, targeting terrorists in response to the deadly attack, the Pakistani information minister said.

title: US, Iran Agree To Halt Fighting "For Now", To Discuss Hormuz Plan In Qatar
content: The back-and-forth military actions over the weekend have tested the barely 11-day-old fragile initial US-Iran agreement that was supposed to halt hostilities during 60 days of negotiations.

title: Monsoon To Arrive In Delhi By July 4, Predicts Weather Office
content: India Meteorological Department forecasts southwest monsoon advance over northern states with heatwave warning in Uttar Pradesh

title: UPI Eases Provident Fund Access, But Don't Rush To Withdraw Yet. Here's Why
content: One of the biggest risks is losing eligibility for lifelong pension benefits that could provide stable monthly income after retirement.

title: 29 Dead As Pakistan Carries Out Ground Operation Along Afghanistan Border
content: The operation comes a day after militants armed with guns and explosives targeted the regional headquarters of the paramilitary Rangers in the southern port city of Karachi, killing three soldiers.

title: 'Certain Shortage': Putin Flags Fuel Crunch After Ukraine Strikes
content: Kyiv calls the attacks fair retribution for Russia's near-daily barrages on Ukrainian civilians and energy infrastructure since its February 2022 offensive.

title: Hope Fades In Venezuela As Death Count Nears 1,500 After Twin Earthquakes
content: Millions more people were feared to lack sanitation and other basic needs after one of Latin America's most devastating earthquake disasters.

title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: Death Count Nears 1,500 After Twin Earthquakes Devastate Venezuela
content: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: Some 774 buildings were badly damaged in back-to-back quakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 that struck on Wednesday evening, including 189 buildings that have totally collapsed

title: "I Will Return To My Country This Year": Sheikh Hasina Exclusive
content: Any attack on the minorities is an attack on the independence of Bangladesh, stressed Sheikh Hasina in an interview with NDTV

title: Bill To Check Thugs In Bengal Assembly On Monday, Allows Preventive Detention
content: The Bill empowers the government to detain a person if it believes detention is necessary to stop future anti-social activity.

title: Qatari National Killed From Shrapnel Injuries Due To "Military Operation"
content: The ministry said the second individual was injured, adding that it located the missing vessel in the early hours of Sunday after search operations that started a day earlier.

title: US-Iran War Highlights: US-Iran Exchange Strikes, Deal Under Pressure
content: US-Iran War Highlights: The clashes brought new tension to the negotiating process meant to end a war launched by the United States and Israel at the end of February.

title: Man Hacked To Death On Bengaluru Street, Revenge Killing Suspected
content: The victim, who has a criminal background, was shifted to a private hospital in a critical condition, where he later succumbed to his injuries.

title: 2 Killed, 2 Injured In Lightning Strikes In Jharkhand
content: Two people, including a three-year-old girl, were killed and two others sustained burn injuries in incidents of lightning strikes in Jharkhand on Sunday, officials said.

title: UDF govt. set to privatise major sectors, says Pinarayi
content: Leader of the Opposition says the UDF government is reversing the State’s gains and that education, health, electricity and even Kerala’s ‘sea and sky’ are headed for privatisation

title: What lay hidden in Chhattisgarh’s Purana Kharve
content: On the banks of the Mahanadi river is Purana Kharve, a village in Chhattisgarh’s Balodabazar-Bhatapara

title: Uttar Pradesh police search residences of all accused in Ayodhya embezzlement case
content: Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh Yadav and Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath trade barbs as the row over alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple donations peaks

title: What does the Centre’s Vision Document on Drug Control 2026-2029 propose?
content: The three-year road map on drug control seeks to dismantle narcotics networks through intelligence-led enforcement, technology, preventive detention, rehabilitation, and coordinated action against inter-State and transnational drug cartels

title: Students stage protest against indiscriminate fee hike in KSLU
content: They highlight the need for corrective measures to resolving various issues affecting students in university in Hubballi

title: DGCA probes incident of trainee pilot getting injured by aircraft propeller at Kanpur airport
content: The incident occurred after the trainee pilot deboarded the twin-engine Tecnam P2006T aircraft VT-NBV, and the instructor concerned has been derostered from flying training duties pending investigation, DGCA said

title: Madras High Court denies anticipatory bail to suspended woman Inspector of Police in gold coin fraud case
content: Justice C. Kumarappan dismisses the advance bail petition filed by Sheela Mary, who had served at the Royapuram All-Women police station in Chennai in 2024

title: Over 2,500 visitors attend vegetable, seeds festival at VIT in Vellore
content: At the event, the Seed Conservator Awards-2026 were presented to nine farmers from six States in recognition of their efforts to conserve traditional seeds

title: Law must act as shield for innocent, not as a sword for the vindictive: SC
content: a bench of justices said trial proceedings cannot be allowed on the basis of vague statements and quashed a corruption case against a govt official in Odisha after noting there was no specific allegation against him . the law must act as shield for the innocent, not as a sword for the vindictive .

title: Government plans more intensive home care for at-risk babies, children
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More

content: PM Modi honoured with 'Guardian of the Blue Horizon' by seychelles . he said both countries stood for more inclusive development . both countries signed nine agreements, including an extradition treaty to combat transnational crime .

title: PM Modi honoured with 'Guardian of the Blue Horizon' by Seychelles
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

content: sheikh Hasina has said she plans to return to Bangladesh "later this year" her rivals termed her remarks a tactic to exert pressure . she also come at a time when political circles in Dhaka are abuzz with reports of a resurgence of her banned Awami League in both rural and urban areas .
